description: Skills registry for skill discovery keywords, background operation rules, and advisory metadata. Validator routing is handled by validator-dispatcher.py.

sf-flow.reason: Flow tests execute in the org and can take several minutes

sf-testing.reason: Apex tests can run for extended periods depending on org size and test volume

sf-deploy.reason: Full deployments and org creation are long-running operations

sf-data.reason: Large data imports can take significant time

sf-datacloud-harmonize.reason: Identity resolution and data-graph refreshes can take several minutes to materialize results

sf-datacloud-segment.reason: Segment publishes and calculated insight runs often execute asynchronously and can take several minutes

sf-datacloud-retrieve.reason: Large Data Cloud query jobs are better handled asynchronously

sf-apex.description: Apex code development with validation and best practices
sf-apex.triggerWhen: user writes, reviews, or fixes Apex classes, triggers, test classes, batch/queueable/schedulable jobs, or touches .cls/.trigger files
sf-apex.doNotTriggerWhen: LWC JavaScript (use sf-lwc), Flow XML (use sf-flow), SOQL-only queries (use sf-soql), or non-Salesforce code

sf-flow.triggerWhen: user builds or edits record-triggered, screen, autolaunched, or scheduled flows, or touches .flow-meta.xml files
sf-flow.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex automation (use sf-apex), process builder migration questions only, or non-Flow declarative config (use sf-metadata)

sf-lwc.description: Lightning Web Components with SLDS compliance
sf-lwc.triggerWhen: user creates/edits LWC components, touches lwc/**/*.js, .html, .css, .js-meta.xml files, or asks about wire service, SLDS, or Jest LWC tests
sf-lwc.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex classes (use sf-apex), Aura components, or Visualforce

sf-ai-agentforce-persona.description: Deep persona design for Agentforce -- 12-attribute framework, brand input, archetype presets, Agent Builder & Agent Script encoding
sf-ai-agentforce-persona.triggerWhen: user designs agent personas, defines agent personality/identity, creates persona documents, encodes persona into Agent Builder fields or Agent Script, translates brand guidelines to agent voice, or asks about agent tone/voice/register
sf-ai-agentforce-persona.doNotTriggerWhen: building agent metadata (use sf-ai-agentforce), testing agents (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or Agent Script DSL (use sf-ai-agentscript)

sf-ai-agentforce.description: Standard Agentforce platform skill for Setup UI agent building, PromptTemplate, Models API, and GenAi metadata
sf-ai-agentforce.triggerWhen: user configures agents in Agent Builder, creates topics/actions, writes PromptTemplates, or touches .genAiFunction/.genAiPlugin/.promptTemplate metadata XML
sf-ai-agentforce.doNotTriggerWhen: Agent Script DSL .agent files (use sf-ai-agentscript), agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or persona design (use sf-ai-agentforce-persona)

sf-metadata.triggerWhen: user creates custom objects, fields, validation rules, or touches .object-meta.xml, .field-meta.xml, .profile-meta.xml files
sf-metadata.doNotTriggerWhen: permission set analysis (use sf-permissions), deploying metadata (use sf-deploy), or Flow XML (use sf-flow)

sf-deploy.triggerWhen: user deploys metadata, creates/manages scratch orgs or sandboxes, sets up CI/CD pipelines, or troubleshoots deployment errors with sf project deploy
sf-deploy.doNotTriggerWhen: writing Apex/LWC code (use sf-apex/sf-lwc), creating metadata XML (use sf-metadata), or querying org data (use sf-data)

sf-testing.triggerWhen: user runs Apex tests, checks code coverage, fixes failing tests, or touches *Test.cls / *_Test.cls files
sf-testing.doNotTriggerWhen: writing Apex production code (use sf-apex), Agentforce agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or Jest/LWC tests (use sf-lwc)

sf-soql.triggerWhen: user writes, optimizes, or debugs SOQL/SOSL queries, touches .soql files, or asks about relationship queries, aggregates, or query performance
sf-soql.doNotTriggerWhen: bulk data operations (use sf-data), Apex DML logic (use sf-apex), or report/dashboard queries

sf-data.triggerWhen: user creates test data, performs bulk import/export, uses sf data CLI commands, or needs data factory patterns for Apex tests
sf-data.doNotTriggerWhen: SOQL query writing only (use sf-soql), Apex test execution (use sf-testing), or metadata deployment (use sf-deploy)

sf-datacloud.description: Cross-phase Salesforce Data Cloud orchestration using the external community sf data360 runtime
sf-datacloud.triggerWhen: user needs a multi-phase Data Cloud workflow, manages data spaces or data kits, troubleshoots Data Cloud across phases, or explicitly references `sf data360` at a broad product level
sf-datacloud.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is isolated to a single Data Cloud phase, standard CRM SOQL, Apex-only work, or STDM/session tracing/parquet telemetry (use sf-ai-agentforce-observability)

sf-datacloud-connect.description: Data Cloud connections, connectors, and source discovery
sf-datacloud-connect.triggerWhen: user manages Data Cloud connections, connectors, source metadata, or tests a connection
sf-datacloud-connect.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is about data streams, DLOs, DMOs, SQL/search, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-prepare.description: Data Cloud ingestion, streams, DLOs, transforms, and Document AI
sf-datacloud-prepare.triggerWhen: user creates or manages Data Cloud streams, DLOs, transforms, or DocAI workflows
sf-datacloud-prepare.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is connector-only setup, harmonization, or pure query/search work

sf-datacloud-harmonize.description: Data Cloud DMOs, mappings, identity resolution, unified profiles, and data graphs
sf-datacloud-harmonize.triggerWhen: user works with Data Cloud DMOs, mappings, identity resolution, unified profiles, data graphs, or universal ID lookup
sf-datacloud-harmonize.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is only about streams/DLOs, segment design, SQL/search, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-segment.description: Data Cloud segments, calculated insights, and audience troubleshooting
sf-datacloud-segment.triggerWhen: user creates or publishes Data Cloud segments, manages calculated insights, or troubleshoots audience logic
sf-datacloud-segment.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is activation delivery, pure query/search work, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-act.description: Data Cloud activations, activation targets, and data actions
sf-datacloud-act.triggerWhen: user manages Data Cloud activations, activation targets, data actions, or downstream delivery setup
sf-datacloud-act.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is segment design, search/query work, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-retrieve.description: Data Cloud SQL, async query, search-index, vector-search, hybrid-search, and metadata introspection workflows
sf-datacloud-retrieve.triggerWhen: user runs Data Cloud SQL, describe, async queries, vector search, hybrid search, search-index workflows, or metadata introspection
sf-datacloud-retrieve.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is CRM SOQL-only, segment design, or STDM/session tracing/parquet analysis

sf-docs.description: Official Salesforce documentation retrieval guidance for hard-to-fetch online docs such as developer.salesforce.com and help.salesforce.com

sf-integration.triggerWhen: user sets up Named Credentials, External Services, REST/SOAP callouts, Platform Events, CDC, or touches .namedCredential-meta.xml files
sf-integration.doNotTriggerWhen: Connected App/OAuth config (use sf-connected-apps), Apex-only logic (use sf-apex), or data import/export (use sf-data)

sf-connected-apps.triggerWhen: user configures OAuth flows, JWT bearer auth, Connected Apps, or touches .connectedApp-meta.xml / .eca-meta.xml files
sf-connected-apps.doNotTriggerWhen: Named Credentials for callouts (use sf-integration), permission policies (use sf-permissions), or API endpoint code (use sf-apex)

sf-debug.triggerWhen: user analyzes debug logs, hits governor limits, reads stack traces, or touches .log files from Salesforce orgs
sf-debug.doNotTriggerWhen: running Apex tests (use sf-testing), fixing Apex code (use sf-apex), or Agentforce session tracing (use sf-ai-agentforce-observability)

sf-diagram-mermaid.triggerWhen: user says 'diagram', 'visualize', 'ERD', or asks for sequence diagrams, flowcharts, class diagrams, or architecture visualizations in Mermaid
sf-diagram-mermaid.doNotTriggerWhen: user wants PNG/SVG image output (use sf-diagram-nanobananapro), or non-Salesforce systems

sf-diagram-nanobananapro.triggerWhen: user asks for PNG/SVG output, UI mockups, wireframes, visual ERDs, or says 'generate image' / 'create mockup'
sf-diagram-nanobananapro.doNotTriggerWhen: text-based Mermaid diagrams (use sf-diagram-mermaid), or non-visual documentation tasks

sf-ai-agentforce-testing.triggerWhen: user tests Agentforce agents, runs sf agent test commands, creates test specs, validates topic routing, or analyzes agent test coverage
sf-ai-agentforce-testing.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex unit tests (use sf-testing), building agents (use sf-ai-agentforce), or Agent Script DSL (use sf-ai-agentscript)

sf-ai-agentscript.description: Agent Script DSL for deterministic Agentforce agents
sf-ai-agentscript.triggerWhen: user writes or edits .agent files, builds FSM-based agents, uses Agent Script CLI (sf agent generate/publish), or asks about deterministic agent patterns
sf-ai-agentscript.doNotTriggerWhen: Setup UI agent building (use sf-ai-agentforce), agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or persona design (use sf-ai-agentforce-persona)

sf-permissions.description: Permission Set analysis and hierarchy visualization
sf-permissions.triggerWhen: user asks 'who has access to X?', analyzes permission sets/groups, or touches .permissionset-meta.xml / .permissionsetgroup-meta.xml files
sf-permissions.doNotTriggerWhen: creating new metadata (use sf-metadata), deploying permission sets (use sf-deploy), or Apex sharing logic (use sf-apex)

sf-ai-agentforce-observability.description: Extract and analyze Agentforce session tracing data from Data Cloud
sf-ai-agentforce-observability.triggerWhen: user extracts STDM data from Data Cloud, analyzes agent session traces, debugs agent conversations via telemetry, or works with .parquet files from Agentforce
sf-ai-agentforce-observability.doNotTriggerWhen: testing agents (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), Apex debug logs (use sf-debug), or building agents (use sf-ai-agentforce)

sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.description: Cross-cutting OmniStudio analysis for namespace detection, dependency mapping, and impact analysis
sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.triggerWhen: user asks about OmniStudio dependencies, wants namespace detection (Core vs vlocity_cmt vs vlocity_ins), needs impact analysis across OmniStudio components, or requests dependency diagrams
sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.doNotTriggerWhen: authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), building FlexCards (use sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard), creating Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or configuring Data Mappers (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper)

sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.description: OmniStudio Data Mapper (formerly DataRaptor) creation and validation with 100-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.triggerWhen: user creates Data Mappers, configures field mappings, works with OmniDataTransform metadata, or asks about DataRaptor/Data Mapper patterns
sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.doNotTriggerWhen: building Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), or analyzing cross-component dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.description: OmniStudio Integration Procedure creation and validation with 110-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.triggerWhen: user creates Integration Procedures, adds DataRaptor steps, configures Remote Actions, or reviews existing IP configurations
sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.doNotTriggerWhen: building OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), creating Data Mappers directly (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper), or analyzing cross-component dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.description: Salesforce Industries Common Core callable Apex generation and review for System.Callable and legacy Open Interface migrations
sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.triggerWhen: user creates or reviews System.Callable classes, migrates VlocityOpenInterface / VlocityOpenInterface2, or builds Industries callable extensions used by OmniStudio, Integration Procedures, or DataRaptors
sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.doNotTriggerWhen: generic Apex classes/triggers (use sf-apex), building Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), configuring Data Mappers (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper), or analyzing namespace/dependency issues (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.description: OmniStudio OmniScript creation and validation with 120-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.triggerWhen: user creates OmniScripts, designs step flows, configures element types, or reviews existing OmniScript configurations
sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.doNotTriggerWhen: building FlexCards (use sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard), creating Integration Procedures directly (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or analyzing dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.description: OmniStudio FlexCard creation and validation with 130-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.triggerWhen: user creates FlexCards, configures data sources, designs card layouts, or asks about OmniUiCard metadata
sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.doNotTriggerWhen: building OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), creating Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or analyzing dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

ohfy-core-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-Core managed package — 143 objects, 37 triggers, bypass patterns, status validations, external IDs, and integration error resolution
ohfy-core-expert.triggerWhen: user writes Tray scripts interacting with Salesforce OHFY objects, debugs integration errors mentioning triggers/validations, builds composite requests with OHFY objects, needs bypass patterns, or works with order status transitions
ohfy-core-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about object schemas only (use ohfy-data-model-expert), OMS/WMS/REX/Ecom-specific logic (use the corresponding ohfy-*-expert skill), or general Salesforce metadata (use sf-metadata)

ohfy-data-model-expert.description: Expert knowledge of the Ohanafy shared data model — 30+ custom objects spanning CRM, supply chain, retail, accounting, and integration tracking
ohfy-data-model-expert.triggerWhen: user asks about Ohanafy object schemas, field definitions, relationships, picklist values, or custom metadata configuration tables
ohfy-data-model-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about trigger/service behavior on these objects (use ohfy-core-expert), general Salesforce metadata patterns (use sf-metadata), or SOQL optimization (use sf-soql)

ohfy-platform-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-Platform — shared services, LWC components, and infrastructure consumed by OMS, WMS, REX, and other SKUs
ohfy-platform-expert.triggerWhen: user works on shared platform services used by multiple SKUs, asks about service locator or dependency injection patterns, or debugs cross-package behavior
ohfy-platform-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about a specific SKU's logic (use ohfy-oms-expert, ohfy-wms-expert, or ohfy-rex-expert), object schemas (use ohfy-data-model-expert), or trigger framework (use ohfy-core-expert)

ohfy-oms-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-OMS — order capture, fulfillment workflows, pricing, returns, and OMS-specific Apex/LWC components
ohfy-oms-expert.triggerWhen: user builds or debugs order capture, fulfillment, pricing, returns, or any OMS-specific feature
ohfy-oms-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about warehouse operations (use ohfy-wms-expert), payment processing (use ohfy-payments-expert), or core trigger framework (use ohfy-core-expert)

ohfy-wms-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-WMS — inventory operations, picking/packing/shipping, lot tracking, location management, and warehouse workflow LWC components
ohfy-wms-expert.triggerWhen: user builds or debugs warehouse operations, inventory management, picking/packing/shipping workflows, lot tracking, or 3PL integrations
ohfy-wms-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about order management (use ohfy-oms-expert), core trigger framework (use ohfy-core-expert), or object schemas (use ohfy-data-model-expert)

ohfy-rex-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-REX — retail execution, POS, display/equipment management, route accounting, DSD operations, and field team mobile workflows
ohfy-rex-expert.triggerWhen: user builds or debugs retail execution features, POS operations, display management, equipment tracking, route accounting, or DSD workflows
ohfy-rex-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about order management (use ohfy-oms-expert), warehouse operations (use ohfy-wms-expert), or object schemas (use ohfy-data-model-expert)

ohfy-ecom-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-Ecom — Shopify/WooCommerce integration, online catalog management, cart/checkout, and e-commerce order processing into Ohanafy
ohfy-ecom-expert.triggerWhen: user builds or debugs e-commerce integrations with Shopify or WooCommerce, works on product sync, cart/checkout flows, or e-commerce order creation
ohfy-ecom-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about order fulfillment after capture (use ohfy-oms-expert), Tray.io workflow patterns (use tray-expert), or Salesforce API patterns (use salesforce-composite)

ohfy-payments-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-Payments — payment capture, authorization, settlement, refunds, gateway integration, and financial transaction tracking
ohfy-payments-expert.triggerWhen: user builds or debugs payment processing, settlement workflows, refund/credit handling, or payment gateway integration in Ohanafy
ohfy-payments-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about order management (use ohfy-oms-expert), core trigger framework (use ohfy-core-expert), or general Apex patterns (use sf-apex)

ohfy-edi-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-EDI — ANSI X12 850/810/856 processing, trading partner management, B2B connector integration, and EDI-to-Salesforce mapping
ohfy-edi-expert.triggerWhen: user works with EDI document processing, X12 parsing or generation, trading partner configuration, or B2B interchange via OpenText/Transcepta/Orderful
ohfy-edi-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about Salesforce-side order processing (use ohfy-core-expert), Tray workflow patterns (use tray-expert), or general API integration (use salesforce-composite)

ohfy-configure-expert.description: Expert knowledge of OHFY-Configure — system configuration, setup wizards, feature flags, post-install handlers, and metadata-driven behavior settings
ohfy-configure-expert.triggerWhen: user sets up or modifies Ohanafy system configuration, works with feature flags, post-install setup, or metadata-driven settings
ohfy-configure-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about core package behavior (use ohfy-core-expert), object schemas (use ohfy-data-model-expert), or general custom metadata patterns (use sf-metadata)

tray-expert.description: Tray.io platform expert — Q&A, workflow design, and integration audit across administration, connectors, Tray Embedded, and best practices
tray-expert.triggerWhen: user asks about Tray.io platform, designs or builds Tray workflows, reviews Tray integration designs, or works on iPaaS architecture
tray-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user writes Tray script JS code (use tray-script-generator), debugs Tray script errors (use tray-errors), needs Tray Custom JS for config wizards (use tray-embedded-customjs), or creates workflow diagrams (use tray-diagrams)

tray-embedded-customjs.description: Generate Custom JavaScript for Tray.ai Embedded config wizards — dynamic dropdowns, slot dependencies, validation, and callConnector patterns
tray-embedded-customjs.triggerWhen: user creates Custom JS for a Tray Embedded solution config slot, builds dynamic dropdowns using callConnector, or customizes config wizard behavior
tray-embedded-customjs.doNotTriggerWhen: user writes exports.step Tray scripts (use tray-script-generator), asks general Tray.io questions (use tray-expert), or debugs workflow errors (use tray-errors)

tray-errors.description: Tray.io error handling patterns — ERROR_TYPES enum, retry strategies, Salesforce-specific error codes, and batch error normalization
tray-errors.triggerWhen: user implements error handling in Tray scripts, processes Salesforce composite/batch API errors, needs ERROR_TYPES constants, or determines retry vs non-retryable errors
tray-errors.doNotTriggerWhen: user generates new Tray scripts from scratch (use tray-script-generator), asks about Tray platform architecture (use tray-expert), or builds composite requests (use salesforce-composite)

tray-diagrams.description: Mermaid diagram generation for Tray.io workflows — flow direction standards, arrow styles, node naming conventions, and architecture visualization
tray-diagrams.triggerWhen: user creates Mermaid diagrams for Tray.io workflows or documents workflow visual architecture
tray-diagrams.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks general Tray.io questions (use tray-expert), generates script code (use tray-script-generator), or creates non-Tray diagrams (use sf-diagram-mermaid)

tray-insights.description: Query Tray Insights API for project usage metrics, execution KPIs, time-series trends, and data-driven workflow prioritization
tray-insights.triggerWhen: user needs Tray project usage data, execution metrics, success/failure rates, or time-series analytics
tray-insights.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks general Tray.io questions (use tray-expert), generates scripts (use tray-script-generator), or creates diagrams without metrics (use tray-diagrams)

tray-script-generator.description: Production-grade Tray.io script generation — patterns, templates, FP rules, Salesforce composite integration, and exports.step scaffolding
tray-script-generator.triggerWhen: user generates new Tray.io exports.step scripts, refactors existing scripts to FP patterns, or debugs Salesforce composite API errors in Tray scripts
tray-script-generator.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about Tray platform architecture (use tray-expert), writes Tray Embedded Custom JS (use tray-embedded-customjs), or needs error handling constants only (use tray-errors)

csv-output.description: Tray CSV output patterns — columns/rows format, csv_data wrapper, fixed-width formatting, and bulk output structures
csv-output.triggerWhen: user generates CSV output from Tray scripts, structures bulk operation results, or needs the csv_data array wrapper pattern
csv-output.doNotTriggerWhen: user generates full Tray scripts (use tray-script-generator) or works on non-Tray CSV files

salesforce-composite.description: Salesforce Composite API patterns — URL encoding for external IDs, composite request field rules, and batch upsert workflows
salesforce-composite.triggerWhen: user builds Salesforce composite or batch API requests, works with external ID upserts, or needs URL encoding for composite URLs
salesforce-composite.doNotTriggerWhen: user writes full Tray scripts (use tray-script-generator), asks about Ohanafy-specific behavior (use ohfy-core-expert), or needs general SF integration patterns (use sf-integration)

salesforce-field-object-creator.description: Generate Tray workflow scripts for creating Salesforce custom objects/fields from CSV — supports all 28 field types
salesforce-field-object-creator.triggerWhen: user provides CSV field specifications for Salesforce metadata creation or needs to automate custom object/field creation via Tray.io Metadata API
salesforce-field-object-creator.doNotTriggerWhen: user creates fields manually in Setup (use sf-metadata), writes Apex code (use sf-apex), or generates non-metadata Tray scripts (use tray-script-generator)

ohfy-transformation-settings.description: Interactive workflow for generating Ohanafy Transformation_Setting__c import CSVs — connects to live org, discovers existing state, computes deltas
ohfy-transformation-settings.triggerWhen: user onboards new product sub-types or UOM combinations, creates transformation settings CSV imports, or asks about UOM conversions in Ohanafy
ohfy-transformation-settings.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks about general Ohanafy configuration (use ohfy-configure-expert) or object schema details (use ohfy-data-model-expert)

deploy-prep.description: Validate Tray scripts against FP patterns, check Salesforce API compliance, validate CSV output format, and generate a deployment checklist
deploy-prep.triggerWhen: user prepares a Tray script for deployment, needs a deployment checklist, or wants to validate functional patterns before production
deploy-prep.doNotTriggerWhen: user deploys Salesforce metadata (use sf-deploy), generates new scripts (use tray-script-generator), or tests scripts interactively (use test-script)

test-script.description: Run Tray script tester with automatic environment validation, dependency installation, and node run.js execution
test-script.triggerWhen: user wants to test a Tray script locally, run node run.js, validate script execution with input.json, or debug script output
test-script.doNotTriggerWhen: user validates for deployment (use deploy-prep), generates new scripts (use tray-script-generator), or runs Salesforce Apex tests (use sf-testing)

security.description: Security and secret management — token rotation, .env protection, credential handling, emergency response protocols, and secure integration patterns
security.triggerWhen: user manages API tokens or credentials, asks about .env file handling, needs token rotation procedures, or responds to a leaked secret
security.doNotTriggerWhen: user works with Salesforce Connected App credentials (use sf-connected-apps), Salesforce FLS/CRUD (use sf-permissions), or general Tray.io auth (use tray-expert)

ukg-expert.description: UKG domain expert — API endpoints, data model, authentication flows, scheduling, timekeeping, time-off, and accruals across Pro HCM, Pro WFM, and Ready
ukg-expert.triggerWhen: user asks about UKG concepts, API details, admin workflows, authentication, scheduling, timekeeping, or data model
ukg-expert.doNotTriggerWhen: user debugs a specific UKG API error (use ukg-api-debug), maps UKG fields to Salesforce (use ukg-field-mapper), or asks about Tray workflow patterns (use tray-expert)

ukg-api-debug.description: Systematic UKG API debugging — auth failures (401/403), rate limits (429), timeouts, sync failures, and missing data troubleshooting
ukg-api-debug.triggerWhen: user reports UKG API errors (401/403/429/400), sync failures, missing data, timeouts, or Tray.io workflow failures related to UKG
ukg-api-debug.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks general UKG questions (use ukg-expert), maps UKG fields to Salesforce (use ukg-field-mapper), or debugs non-UKG Tray errors (use tray-errors)

ukg-field-mapper.description: UKG-to-Salesforce field mapping — generate mapping tables, list available UKG fields by entity, validate mappings, and flag product-specific differences
ukg-field-mapper.triggerWhen: user generates or validates field mappings between UKG API responses and Salesforce objects, or asks what fields UKG returns
ukg-field-mapper.doNotTriggerWhen: user asks general UKG questions (use ukg-expert), debugs UKG API errors (use ukg-api-debug), or works on Ohanafy schemas (use ohfy-data-model-expert)

org-connect.description: Connect to a live Salesforce org, retrieve metadata, detect installed OHFY packages, and generate an org snapshot for debugging
org-connect.triggerWhen: user wants to connect to a Salesforce org for debugging, asks about what is deployed, or needs to understand org state
org-connect.doNotTriggerWhen: user works purely with source code (use ohfy-* skills), asks about Salesforce concepts generically (use sf-* skills), or generates transformation settings (use ohfy-transformation-settings)

claude-code-best-practices.description: Reference guide for Claude Code best practices — skill authoring, subagent patterns, memory management, MCP configuration, and multi-agent orchestration
claude-code-best-practices.triggerWhen: user creates or improves skills/agents/commands, designs multi-agent orchestration, or reviews CLAUDE.md patterns
claude-code-best-practices.doNotTriggerWhen: user works on Salesforce code (use sf-* skills), integration code (use tray-*/ohfy-* skills), or debugs live org issues (use org-connect)
